Name: Infront
Short Description: Infront has partnered with Spalk to support World Feed and multilingual commentary production on more than 10 different sports properties. From Speed Skating to Triathlon, Downhill Skiing to Handball, Infront leverage Spalk’s technology to make sure every fan around the world can enjoy their favorite sport.
Title: What rights maximization looks like across 10 sports properties
Rich Text Content:
Infront manages some of the most complex rights portfolios in international sport. Across the 10 properties Infront works with Spalk on, the challenge is coordinating signal delivery, rights-compliant distribution and a growing multilingual output, all from a single operational hub.

Spalk integrates directly into Infront's Milan MCR, embedding into their existing master control infrastructure. That integration is what makes the workflow scalable with signals, coordination, and quality checks flow through a single point of contact, reducing the operational overhead of managing multiple properties simultaneously.

On game day, signals arrive via SRT and Spalk takes responsibility for distribution onward to TV networks, OTT platforms and social media streaming destinations. Each feed is routed according to the specific rights arrangement for that league. No two properties are identical, and Spalk’s system flexes to accommodate each one.

While the majority of feeds are English World Feeds, Infront is producing an increasing volume of multilingual commentary to unlock new markets and grow the underlying value of their rights. Spalk's Talent Marketplace provides the infrastructure to scale that output by identifying commentators by language, sport, and market familiarity, and managing contracting and payment in the same workflow.

For a rights management business, the commercial logic is straightforward: more languages mean more markets, and more markets mean more value. Spalk is how Infront is building that capability without building a new operation to run it.

‍
Challenge: Multi-property signal delivery and rights-compliant distribution at scale
Solution: Virtual Commentary Studio + Talent Marketplace + Managed distribution
Outcome: Scalable multilingual output across 10 properties from a single workflow
Testimonial: "Handling the international distribution and organization of a multi-destination feed with localized commentary with the support of Spalk has never been so easy. Reaction time from actual request to delivery has been as short as 48 hours, which means being able to support our clients with solutions to requirements or problems in a very short time."

Name: FIBA
Short Description: FIBA streams over 1,000 youth matches annually to 30 million fans. Spalk handles the multilingual commentary layer - enriching every broadcast with local-language coverage while keeping production costs in check.
Title: Supporting the global reach of basketball
Rich Text Content:
FIBA runs some of the most ambitious live basketball programming in the world. Across FIBA 3x3, FIBA Africa, FIBA Asia, and FIBA Youth Championships, the challenge isn't just producing great broadcasts, it's doing it consistently while working across time zones, languages and local broadcast partners who all have different requirements.

Spalk's role starts before tip-off. Each event begins with structured testing alongside the onsite broadcast team to align signal workflows and confirm delivery before anyone is on the court. That coordination is quiet, technical, and essential, and makes the live broadcast on game day feel seamless.

Talent sourcing happens through Spalk's Marketplace. For FIBA properties, that means identifying basketball commentators who know the players, understand the league culture, and can deliver broadcast-quality English and French commentary from wherever they are in the world. Contracting and payment flows through the same platform, removing the administrative overhead that typically comes with managing international freelance talent.

Finished commentary is distributed across YouTube, WSC and broadcast rightsholders ensuring the right audio reaches the right audience on the right platform.
Challenge: Multilingual commentary at scale across global properties
Solution: Spalk Talent Marketplace + Virtual Commentary Studio
Outcome: Consistent English & French delivery across FIBA events
Testimonial: "FIBA has worked very hard to build our live streaming offering. We are now streaming more than 1,000 Youth Matches every year, for over 30 million basketball fans. Spalk has helped us to localize and enrich our live stream experience while also keeping production cost under control."

Name: Sky Switzerland
Short Description: Spalk supports Sky Switzerland with multilingual coverage across a variety of sports and leagues that they cover. With four national languages, it is important for broadcasters in the Swiss Market to reach out to every home and sports fan!
Title: Localizing football coverage for the Swiss market
Rich Text Content:
Sky Switzerland operates in one of the most linguistically complex broadcast markets in Europe. Serving French, German and Italian-speaking audiences demands a production infrastructure built for precision.

Spalk's integration with Sky Switzerland began with substantial technical groundwork. Signal workflows were tested rigorously alongside their broadcast team to validate compatibility, drive latency below one second, and ensure strict compliance with Sky's specific PID and audio channel mapping requirements.

Across three football leagues, Spalk now sources and deploys experienced commentators fluent in French, German, and Italian through our Talent Marketplace. Each commentator is matched not just by language but by their familiarity with the leagues and the clubs their audience cares about. The workflow runs consistently, week in, week out, across the full league calendar.

In 2026, the partnership has expanded into localized graphics production. Using HTML5 overlays delivered directly into the live broadcast, Sky Switzerland can now run fully localized on-screen production with market-specific branding, local sponsor integrations, and graphics that feel native to each language audience. That capability opens new sponsorship inventory and enhanced the broadcast further.

Distribution is delivered via SRT directly to Sky Switzerland's CDN layer with a clean, low-overhead path that keeps latency tight and gives Sky full control of the downstream delivery chain.
Challenge: Multilingual localization across three language markets
Solution: Virtual Commentary Studio + HTML5 graphics + Talent Marketplace
Outcome: Fully localized live production with new sponsorship inventory
Testimonial: "Being able to localise the content to our viewers has allowed us to grow the overall viewership on our transmissions and build a deeper connection to our subscribers. We are positively surprised by the outcome and the expertise of the production with Spalk. During the next years, subscribers will be able to enjoy more and more events in their own language."
